title: Transcript Pointer (structured capability)
description: A validated capability that names a predecessor's per-agent transcript and binds it to the integrity it had at spawn time. A pointer is opaque to any lead (R11): only a continuation peer or the disposable bn-consult-extractor ever reads the transcript it names, and only after validate(pointer, root) confirms shape + project-root-hash match + file existence + hash + size BEFORE any read (R18). The transcript itself is treated as opaque text (DI2/R15); nothing here describes the transcript's internal schema.

agent_id.description: Spawn-time id of the predecessor agent. Becomes the agent-<id>.jsonl filename segment via locate-transcript resolution; must contain no path separators or traversal.

session_id.description: Id of the Claude Code session the predecessor ran under. Carried for audit/attribution (R23); the resolved session path is supplied separately to validate().

project_root_hash.description: SHA-256 of the absolute project root the predecessor ran against. Re-derived from the validating root and compared; a mismatch means the pointer was minted against a different repo/checkout and must be refused before any read.

spawn_timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp at which the predecessor was spawned. Audit field (R23); not load-bearing for integrity (the file hash is).

file_hash.description: SHA-256 of the transcript file's bytes as observed at spawn/handoff time. Re-computed at validation and compared; any drift means the transcript changed since the pointer was minted and the transcript is no longer authoritative (R18).

byte_size.description: Byte size of the transcript file at spawn/handoff time. A cheap pre-hash integrity gate; a size mismatch fails the pointer with a precise reason before the file is hashed. Hashes are SHA-256 (the only supported algorithm).
